message:
Add hostname resolution to accept_tcp (#6017)

## 🔍 Problem

- `accept_tcp` no longer matched `load_tcp` for peer hostname
enrichment.
- `$peer.ip` in the new TCP operators was exposed as a string instead of
an
  `ip` value.

## 🛠️ Solution

- Add an async reverse-DNS helper in `libtenzir/async/`.
- Add `resolve_hostnames=true` to `accept_tcp` and expose
`$peer.hostname`.
- Make `$peer.ip` use the native `ip` type in both `accept_tcp` and
  `from_tcp`.
- Add integration coverage.

## 💬 Review

- Focus on the async lookup boundary and message flow in `accept_tcp`.
- The helper currently uses `spawn_blocking(...)`, so it avoids blocking
the
EventBase and operator state machine while staying reusable for a future
  `from_udp` port.

## Documentation PR

- https://github.com/tenzir/docs/pull/258

<sub>resolves TNZ-498</sub>

files.patch:
@@ -64,6 +64,9 @@ code should follow the new conventions.
   Instead, prefer cancellation through `folly::CancellationSource`, or if the
   other task should not be cancelled, a proper async notification mechanism (for
   example: event in an async queue, or `Notify`).
+- Prefer `Notify` over direct `folly::coro::Baton` use in coroutine code.
+  `Baton` is not cancellation-aware and should be reserved for low-level
+  primitives that provide their own cancellation integration.
 - Atomics and mutexes are a last resort. When unavoidable, keep the shared
   surface minimal and document why a queue does not work.
 - See `executor.md` for operator-specific patterns.

@@ -124,10 +124,24 @@ when the operator needs a separate shutdown path to behave correctly. For
 example, a HTTP operator that must stop accepting connections should stop the
 `accept` loop with a custom `folly::CancellationSource`.
 
+When you pass a custom token to `co_withCancellation`, merge it with the
+ambient token from `co_current_cancellation_token`. Otherwise, the custom token
+replaces outer pipeline or executor cancellation for that await.
+
 ```cpp
-ctx.spawn_task(folly::coro::co_withCancellation(cancel_.getToken(), accept_loop()));
+ctx.spawn_task([this]() -> Task<void> {
+  auto token = folly::cancellation_token_merge(
+    co_await folly::coro::co_current_cancellation_token,
+    cancel_.getToken());
+  co_await folly::coro::co_withCancellation(token, accept_loop());
+});
 ```
 
+Pass a bare custom token only when you intentionally want to shield the await
+from outer cancellation. If local shutdown and outer cancellation need
+different behavior, keep both tokens available so you can tell which one fired
+and propagate only the outer cancellation.
+
 Do not add a second `stop_` flag—cancellation tokens already express this.
 
 Usually, you do not need to check for cancellation explicitly, as many async

@@ -332,7 +332,9 @@ else ()
   dependency_summary("yaml-cpp" yaml-cpp "Dependencies")
 endif ()
 
-# Link against c-ares.
+# Link against c-ares. The core async DNS utilities live in libtenzir, while
+# the legacy dns_lookup builtin still uses c-ares directly.
+target_link_libraries(libtenzir PRIVATE c-ares::cares)
 target_link_libraries(libtenzir_builtins PRIVATE c-ares::cares)
 string(APPEND TENZIR_FIND_DEPENDENCY_LIST "\nfind_package(c-ares REQUIRED)")
 dependency_summary("c-ares" c-ares::cares "Dependencies")

@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+---
+fixtures:
+  - tcp:
+      mode: client
+timeout: 60
+---
+
+accept_tcp env("TCP_ENDPOINT"), resolve_hostnames=true {
+  read_lines
+  has_hostname = $peer.hostname != null
+  peer_ip = $peer.ip
+}
+select has_hostname, peer_ip
+head 1

@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+---
+fixtures:
+  - tcp:
+      mode: server
+assertions:
+  fixtures:
+    tcp:
+      server_sent_contains: "foo"
+timeout: 60
+---
+
+from_tcp env("TCP_ENDPOINT") {
+  read_lines
+  peer_ip = $peer.ip
+}
+select peer_ip
+head 1
